title: Of Arthour and of Merlin
summary: The repository artifact is a pinned copy of University of Michigan CME00133, which encodes selected pages of Eugen Kölbing's 1890 Arthour and Merlin. The encoding reproduces Kölbing's title page, the Auchinleck text on printed pp. 3-272, and the parallel younger-version streams on pp. 275-370 (L/P composite) and pp. 275-355 (D). Kölbing transcribed the older version directly from NLS Adv.MS.19.2.1, used Lincoln's Inn Hale 150 as the principal younger L base, and transcribed Bodleian MS Douce 236 as a separate D-family stream. For text supplied from the Percy Folio he explicitly used Hales and Furnivall's 1867-68 printed transcript, and his Harley 6223 readings pass through Turnbull's 1838 edition; neither printed intermediary is collapsed into a direct manuscript edge. Complete public copies of Kölbing, Hales/Furnivall, and Turnbull are cached, as are complete provider-exposed facsimiles of the Auchinleck Manuscript and Hale 150. Digital Bodleian publishes only three selected Douce 236 openings, all overlapping the work but constituting partial manuscript coverage. No complete public facsimile was found for Douce 236, Add MS 27879, or Harley MS 6223, so current catalog, physical-access, and reproduction-request routes are recorded.
review_notes: The primary chain is repository artifact -> U-M encoding -> Kölbing 1890 edition -> manuscripts and named printed intermediaries.
The XML's first global DIV1 is the editorial title page. The Auchinleck text is DIV1[2], and the younger supplement is DIV1[3] with L/P and D as DIV2[1] and DIV2[2].

Kölbing's L stream is composite: Hale 150 supplies the surviving base through his verse 1,980, while P supplies a missing leaf and the terminal 612-line continuation through the Hales/Furnivall transcript. P and H also contribute selected apparatus readings.
The current Hale 150 work locus is ff. 11r-27v. Kölbing's legacy sequence f. 13, ff. 2-3, ff. 14-27v maps to current f. 11, ff. 12-13, ff. 14-27v and is preserved in both witness and relation scopes.

For Douce 236, Kölbing and DIMEV give ff. 1-36v, whereas the current Bodleian catalogue infers the item across ff. 1r-37v. The relation uses the historical source locator and qualifies the current boundary rather than asserting an exact one-to-one crosswalk.
The three Digital Bodleian surfaces are a partial selection, not a complete witness facsimile. Their exact labels are fol. 013v-14r, fol. 021v-022r, and fol. 022v-023r.
Turnbull's 1838 Auchinleck edition is a consulted predecessor and comparison source, not the base of Kölbing's main text; its distinct Harley-fragment role remains explicit.

The Internet Archive Kölbing PDF scans a 1968 Rodopi facsimile reissue containing the 1890 edition. The reissue/scan is a separate facsimile entity so its modern wrapper and uncertain scan rights are not conflated with the public-domain 1890 editorial text.

entities.label: General Corpus repository artifact CME00133.xml
entities.description: The fixed XML source artifact for CME00133 in the pinned CME submodule.
notes: The file is 837,823 bytes; IDG, BIBNO, and VID all identify CME00133.
Global DIV1[1] is the title-page paratext, DIV1[2] the Auchinleck poem, and DIV1[3] the younger supplement with two DIV2 streams.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse encoding CME00133
entities.description: The U-M manually keyed electronic text represented by stable item identifier CME00133 and preserved in the repository artifact.
entities.reproduction_format: SGML/XML transcription with structural, printed-page, manuscript-folio, line-number, note, and typographic markup
notes: The encoding selects the 1890 title page, pp. 3-272, the p. 273 section heading, and the parallel text beginning p. 275. Kölbing's introduction, blank pp. 2 and 274, Lovelich appendix, notes, register, and glossary are outside the continuous encoded text.
Page and folio milestones preserve Kölbing's printed alignment and historical manuscript references.

entities.label: Eugen Kölbing's 1890 Arthour and Merlin edition
entities.description: A scholarly edition of the Auchinleck version followed by a parallel younger-version edition using L/P/H and D, with introduction, Lovelich appendix, textual notes, register, and glossary.

bibliographic.citation: Eugen Kölbing, ed., Arthour and Merlin, nach der Auchinleck-Hs. Nebst zwei Beilagen, Altenglische Bibliothek 4 (Leipzig: O. R. Reisland, 1890).
bibliographic.title: Arthour and Merlin, nach der Auchinleck-Hs. Nebst zwei Beilagen

notes: The Auchinleck text is printed on pp. 1-272; the younger parallel version on pp. 273-370; Lovelich on pp. 371-408; annotations and reference matter on pp. 409-503.

entities.reproduction_format: Manuscript-based critical/diplomatic edition with abbreviation expansions, normalized capitalization and word division, parallel witness streams, and apparatus notes
notes: Kölbing says the Auchinleck text closely follows the manuscript and that he re-collated proofs against it in situ.
L is his base for the younger class-a stream; D is printed separately; substantive P/H readings appear in notes and P supplies missing L passages through Hales/Furnivall.
The Lovelich specimen from CCCC MS 80 and Mary Bateson's collaboration are outside CME00133's encoded literary streams.

entities.label: 1968 Rodopi facsimile reissue and Internet Archive scan of Kölbing 1890
entities.description: A complete scan of the 1968 Rodopi facsimile reissue, retaining its modern wrapper and the complete 1890 edition within it.

bibliographic.citation: Eugen Kölbing, Arthour and Merlin, 1890; facsimile reissue (Amsterdam: Rodopi, 1968), digitized by Internet Archive.
bibliographic.title: Arthour and Merlin, nach der Auchinleck-Hs. Nebst zwei Beilagen

entities.reproduction_format: Complete Internet Archive PDF, OCR text, and item metadata
notes: The 1968 wrapper is not part of the 1890 edition and is not encoded in CME00133.
The cached PDF passed qpdf validation and contains all 704 pages exposed by the provider.

entities.label: National Library of Scotland, Advocates MS 19.2.1 (Auchinleck Manuscript)
entities.description: A major Middle English literary codex made probably in London in 1331-1340; Scribe 1 copied Arthour and Merlin at current ff. 201rb-256vb.

holding.notes: The work occupies current ff. 201rb-256vb; Kölbing's four-column notation abbreviates this as 201b-256b.

notes: The opening miniature was cut out, damaging verses 34-44.
A lost leaf after f. 254 removes about 172 verses after the position numbered 9,446.
The extant/encoded text has 9,763 actual lines; Kölbing's editorial positions reach 9,938 and DIMEV preserves an additional 9,772-line source count.
The last 6,774 lines are unique to this witness according to the NLS headnote.

entities.label: Lincoln's Inn, Hale MS 150
entities.description: A Middle English romance codex whose Arthour and Merlin copy occupies current ff. 11r-27v and is the principal base of Kölbing's younger class-a stream.

date_statements.label: Production
date_statements.notes: The NLS headnote supplies the c.1450 date; Lincoln's Inn's broad viewer description instead calls the hand fourteenth-century.

holding.notes: Current work locus ff. 11r-27v. Kölbing's legacy sequence f. 13, ff. 2-3, ff. 14-27v maps respectively to current f. 11, ff. 12-13, and ff. 14-27v.

notes: The complete public object exposes 264 images including binding and end matter.
The work begins on public image 29 and ends with Explicit Merlin on image 62.
Kölbing says L is missing a leaf between his old ff. 3 and 14 and that its own text reaches his verse 1,980; P supplies the lacuna and terminal continuation.
The Shropshire localization is linguistic/textual; it is not treated as an independently proven place of codex manufacture.

entities.label: Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS Douce 236
entities.description: A fifteenth-century English parchment codex containing an Arthour and Merlin version imperfect at both ends and with a gap after f. 23.

holding.notes: The current catalogue infers the item at ff. 1r-37v; Kölbing and DIMEV identify their text as ff. 1-36v. The terminal discrepancy has not been resolved by a complete facsimile.

notes: Written in Anglicana; the current catalogue records about 1,300 lines and about 26 lost at the opening.
A gap follows f. 23.
Kölbing prints this as the separate D-family stream on pp. 275-355.
Only three selected openings are public; they overlap the work but do not establish the whole item boundary.

entities.label: British Library, Add MS 27879 (Percy Folio)
entities.description: The mid-seventeenth-century Percy Folio, containing Arthour and Merlin on manuscript pp. 145-178; its transcript supplies L's lacuna and terminal continuation in Kölbing's younger stream.

holding.notes: Arthour and Merlin occupies manuscript pp. 145-178; the current catalogue requires a letter of introduction for access.

notes: No public manuscript facsimile was exposed by the current BL record on 2026-07-13.
Kölbing explicitly cites the Hales/Furnivall printed P transcript, not an unambiguously documented direct inspection of this codex.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 6223
entities.description: A paper historical miscellany containing John Stow's short Arthour and Merline excerpt at current ff. 2r-2v, historically cited as ff. 123r-v.

holding.notes: Current locus ff. 2r-2v; Kölbing, Turnbull, and DIMEV preserve historical f. 123r-v.

notes: The BL catalogue and Kölbing describe 62 lines; DIMEV records 61, probably reflecting a counting convention that remains unresolved.
No public manuscript facsimile was exposed by the current BL record on 2026-07-13.

entities.label: Hales and Furnivall's Bishop Percy's Folio Manuscript, volume 1
entities.description: The printed transcript of the Percy Folio's ballads and romances through which Kölbing obtained P-supplied Arthour and Merlin passages.

bibliographic.citation: John W. Hales and Frederick J. Furnivall, eds., Bishop Percy's Folio Manuscript: Ballads and Romances, vol. 1 (London: N. Trübner, 1867-68).
bibliographic.title: Bishop Percy's Folio Manuscript: Ballads and Romances

entities.reproduction_format: Printed manuscript transcript with editorial notes
notes: The full Arthur/Merlin component occupies pp. 401-496; its introduction is pp. 401-416, the P transcript begins on p. 417, and Kölbing specifically cites pp. 422-496 for the passages corresponding to his P supply.
Kölbing says he silently corrected small inaccuracies in the printed transcript, but his wording does not securely establish direct codex inspection.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Hales and Furnivall volume 1
entities.description: A complete Internet Archive scan contributed by the University of Toronto's Robarts Library.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete PDF, OCR text, and Internet Archive item metadata

entities.label: Turnbull's 1838 Arthour and Merlin edition
entities.description: The first printed Auchinleck edition, also printing John Stow's Harley 6223 fragment in its introduction; Kölbing critiques and consults it but does not use it as his main-text base.

bibliographic.citation: W. B. D. D. Turnbull, ed., Arthour and Merlin: A Metrical Romance (Edinburgh: Abbotsford Club, 1838).
bibliographic.title: Arthour and Merlin: A Metrical Romance

entities.reproduction_format: Printed manuscript edition with introductory Harley-fragment transcript
notes: The Harley fragment appears on introductory pp. x-xiii.
Kölbing identifies multiple Turnbull errors and says he newly collated his own proofs with the Auchinleck manuscript.

entities.label: NLS/Internet Archive scan of Turnbull 1838
entities.description: A complete scan supplied by the National Library of Scotland and distributed through its derivative server and Internet Archive.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete PDF, OCR text, and Internet Archive item metadata

entities.label: NLS complete public IIIF facsimile of the Auchinleck Manuscript
entities.description: The complete 734-canvas NLS IIIF object, cached at a non-upscaling 1600-pixel-wide access profile with its exact Presentation manifest preserved separately.

notes: Target work surfaces are canvases 437-548, corresponding to ff. 201r-256v; text begins at f. 201rb and ends at f. 256vb.

entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and 734 JPEG access derivatives

entities.label: Lincoln's Inn complete published image inventory for Hale MS 150
entities.description: A complete cache of all 264 images publicly exposed by Lincoln's Inn, including binding, end matter, leaves, and back cover.

notes: Target work images 29-62 show current ff. 11r-27v.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian three-surface selection from MS Douce 236
entities.description: A three-canvas Digital Bodleian object made from 35mm slides. Every exposed surface overlaps Arthour and Merlin, but the selection is partial relative to both the work and manuscript.

notes: The exact surfaces are fol. 013v-14r, fol. 021v-022r, and fol. 022v-023r.

entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and three full-size JPEG images
notes: This entity is not a whole-codex or whole-work facsimile.

entities.label: Digital Index of Middle English Verse version 1.0 records 2807 and 1886
entities.description: DIMEV records the older Auchinleck incipit and younger couplet-version incipit separately while cross-referencing them and listing the NLS, L, D, P, and H witnesses and editions.
entities.reproduction_format: Structured XML authority, manuscript, and record data distributed in the version 1.0 ZIP
notes: Record 1003, A Treatise of the Birth and Prophecy of Merlin, is only cross-referenced and is not merged into this transmission graph.

Digital Index of Middle English Verse

subject.description: Encoded title page and three literary streams

object.description: 1890 title page; Auchinleck reading text; younger heading and parallel L/P and D reading texts

scope.notes: The internal p. 1 heading, blank pp. 2 and 274, and the 1968 reissue wrapper are excluded; the main reading text begins on printed p. 3.

object.description: Arthour and Merlin copied by Scribe 1

scope.notes: Kölbing says the text generally follows the manuscript closely and that the proofs were re-collated against it in situ.

subject.description: L-base portions of the younger class-a stream through Kölbing verse 1,980, excluding the missing leaf and P-supplied continuation
locators: numbered verses through 1,980, with an internal P supply

scope.notes: The legacy/current foliation crosswalk is based on direct image sequencing and is retained because Kölbing cites the older order.

subject.description: P passages inserted into the L stream: the missing-leaf supply and the 612-line continuation after L ends
locators: terminal continuation after Kölbing verse 1,980 through XML line 2,492

object.description: Printed P transcript used for the supplied passages

scope.notes: Kölbing names Hales/Furnivall's print and says he silently corrected small inaccuracies; this edge deliberately stops at the printed intermediary.

subject.description: Substantive P apparatus readings in the L/P/H family

object.description: Arthour and Merlin transcript from P
Percy Folio Arthour and Merlin copy

subject.description: H readings reported in the younger L/P/H apparatus

scope.notes: Kölbing explicitly says the H fragment is known through Turnbull and identifies Turnbull's errors; no direct codex edge is asserted.

subject.description: Introduction and critical comparison for the Auchinleck version

scope.notes: Consultation does not make Turnbull the textual base; Kölbing criticizes it and re-transcribes the manuscript.

subject.description: 1838 main printed Arthour and Merlin text

scope.notes: The direct transcription edge follows Kölbing's historical ff. 1-36v source locator. The current catalogue's inferred ff. 1r-37v boundary is preserved separately and is not claimed as an exact crosswalk without a complete facsimile.

subject.description: Complete 1890 edition reproduced inside the 1968 reissue and 704-page scan

rights.component: Repository XML serialization, markup, and compilation
rights.basis: The pinned CME repository's Unlicense file expressly dedicates 'software' but does not clearly state whether the corpus XML data and editorial markup fall within that scope. The medieval and 1890 textual layers are assessed separately.
notes: Do not infer XML-level reuse rights solely from the public-domain status of the medieval wording or Kölbing edition.

rights.component: U-M keyed electronic text and collection presentation
rights.basis: The U-M collection describes the keyed texts as derived from public-domain editions and marks the collection No Copyright - United States.
notes: NoC-US is a rights statement, not a worldwide license.

rights.component: 1890 editorial text, notes, typography, and organization
rights.jurisdiction: United States and jurisdictions applying life plus 70 years
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1890 and Kölbing died in 1899. This assessment concerns the original 1890 edition, not a modern wrapper, scan, OCR, or repository encoding.

rights.component: 1968 Rodopi reissue wrapper, scan images, PDF assembly, and OCR
rights.basis: The scan includes a 1968 facsimile wrapper and modern digitization layers. The cached Internet Archive metadata supplies no licenseurl or rights field, so the public-domain status of the 1890 edition cannot be generalized to those layers.

rights.component: Medieval manuscript wording of Arthour and Merlin
rights.basis: The manuscript dates to 1331-1340. This assessment concerns medieval wording only, not modern catalogue descriptions, photography, or transcriptions.

rights.component: NLS digital images and IIIF presentation
rights.basis: The exact NLS IIIF manifest applies the Creative Commons Public Domain Mark 1.0 and states that the object is free of known copyright restrictions.
notes: The Public Domain Mark is a status label, not a license. Attribution and provider citation remain good scholarly practice.

rights.component: Medieval manuscript wording of Arthour and Merlin
rights.basis: The manuscript is medieval. This assessment concerns the wording only and does not extend to Lincoln's Inn photographs, website text, or downloadable files.

rights.component: Lincoln's Inn digital images and website text
rights.jurisdiction: Worldwide license grant, subject to license terms
rights.basis: Lincoln's Inn's Digital Collections Terms of Use assert ownership of the images and text and make the content available under CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 for non-commercial use.
notes: Commercial reuse requires separate contact with Lincoln's Inn; the no-derivatives condition must be assessed before transformation or redistribution.

rights.component: Medieval manuscript wording of Arthour and Merlin
rights.basis: The manuscript dates to the fifteenth century. This assessment concerns medieval wording only, not modern catalogue encoding, photography, or supplied scans.

rights.component: Digital Bodleian photographs of the three selected openings
rights.jurisdiction: Worldwide license grant, subject to license terms
rights.basis: The exact IIIF manifest states Photo © Bodleian Libraries and identifies Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International.

rights.component: Any new scans or photographs supplied by Bodleian services
rights.basis: Access or delivery does not itself establish publication or redistribution rights. Exact terms depend on the service, quote, order conditions, delivered-file notice, and any written permission.

rights.component: Historical manuscript wording on pp. 145-178
rights.basis: The manuscript copy dates to 1630-1670. This assessment concerns historical wording only, not modern catalogue text, photography, or a supplied reproduction.

rights.component: Any new British Library manuscript photographs or scans
rights.basis: BL Digitisation Services assesses copying and licensing separately; exact reuse terms depend on the quote, order, delivered files, and permissions granted.

rights.component: John Stow's sixteenth-century manuscript excerpt
rights.basis: The excerpt was copied in the sixteenth century and John Stow died in 1605. This assessment does not extend to modern catalogue text, photography, or a supplied reproduction.

rights.component: Any new British Library manuscript photographs or scans
rights.basis: BL Digitisation Services assesses copying and licensing separately; exact reuse terms depend on the quote, order, delivered files, and permissions granted.

rights.component: 1867-68 printed transcript, editorial text, and organization
rights.jurisdiction: United States and jurisdictions applying life plus 70 years
rights.basis: The volume was published in 1867-68 and both editors died more than seventy years ago. This assessment excludes the modern scan and OCR layers.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan images, PDF assembly, and OCR
rights.basis: The cached Internet Archive metadata contains no licenseurl or rights field. The underlying edition is public domain, but the manifest does not infer a license for the scan and OCR layers.

rights.component: 1838 printed edition, introduction, and organization
rights.jurisdiction: United States and jurisdictions applying life plus 70 years
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1838 and Turnbull died in 1863. This assessment excludes the modern scan and OCR layers.

rights.component: NLS/Internet Archive digital scan and distributed derivatives
rights.jurisdiction: Scotland and worldwide license grant, subject to license terms
rights.basis: The exact Internet Archive item metadata supplies Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 2.5 Scotland as licenseurl.

rights.component: DIMEV version 1.0 database contents and XML distribution
rights.jurisdiction: Worldwide license grant, subject to license terms
rights.basis: The version 1.0 research-data release is distributed under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International.

editorial_practices.representation: Expanded letters and syllables printed in italic type
editorial_practices.description: Kölbing expands manuscript abbreviations and marks supplied expansion characters typographically; CME00133 preserves those italics with HI elements.
editorial_practices.scope: Auchinleck, L/P, and D reading texts

editorial_practices.representation: Editorial capitals at sentence openings and proper names
editorial_practices.description: Kölbing says he introduced capitals at sentence beginnings and for proper names rather than mechanically reproducing manuscript case.
editorial_practices.scope: Auchinleck, L/P, and D reading texts

editorial_practices.representation: Inseparable prefixes and prepositions joined editorially
editorial_practices.description: Kölbing says he joins inseparable prefixes and prepositions even where the manuscript separates them. This is recorded under the closest available schema practice; it concerns spacing/word division, not an inference that all punctuation was normalized.
editorial_practices.scope: Auchinleck, L/P, and D reading texts

editorial_practices.source_feature: Auchinleck manuscript wording and damaged/lost loci
editorial_practices.representation: Directly transcribed text with editorial notes, gap-like dot lines, folio milestones, and retained inferred numbering
editorial_practices.description: Kölbing prepared the main text from the Auchinleck manuscript, compared Turnbull's predecessor edition, and re-collated proofs against the codex in situ.
editorial_practices.scope: Kölbing pp. 1-272; CME reading selection pp. 3-272

editorial_practices.source_feature: Two younger-version families represented by L/P/H and D
editorial_practices.representation: L-based class-a stream with P/H variants and P supplies, printed in parallel with a separately transcribed D stream
editorial_practices.description: Kölbing groups L, P, and H as one family and D as another. L is his class-a base; substantive P/H variants appear in notes, readings agreeing with D are starred, and D remains a separate continuous stream.
editorial_practices.scope: Kölbing pp. 275-370 L/P and pp. 275-355 D

editorial_practices.source_feature: L's missing leaf and termination at Kölbing verse 1,980
editorial_practices.representation: P text inserted at the L lacuna and for the final 612-line continuation, through Hales/Furnivall's transcript
editorial_practices.description: The printed L/P stream is a composite rather than a wholly direct L transcription. The interpolation points and intermediary are retained as scoped relations.
editorial_practices.scope: Selected passages within the L/P stream, printed pp. 275-370

editorial_practices.source_feature: Kölbing's full 1890 edition and the additional 1968 wrapper in the scanned reissue
editorial_practices.representation: Title page plus selected continuous reading texts, headings, notes, page/folio markers, and typography
editorial_practices.description: The electronic text selects the 1890 title page, Auchinleck reading text on pp. 3-272, the younger heading, and the L/P and D reading streams beginning p. 275. It excludes the internal p. 1 heading, blank pp. 2 and 274, introduction, Lovelich appendix, back matter, and 1968 wrapper.

evidence.citation: General Corpus pinned CME00133 XML artifact
evidence.summary: Identifies CME00133 and encodes the 1890 title page, Auchinleck reading text, and the two parallel younger-version streams with Kölbing's page, folio, line-number, note, and typographic markup.

evidence.citation: Local structural and boundary inspection of CME00133.xml, 2026-07-13
evidence.locator: FRONT and BODY; global DIV1[1-3] and DIV2[1-2]
evidence.summary: Established the global division order and exact element counts: 3 DIV1, 2 DIV2, 13,535 L, 412 LG, 4 P, 429 PB, 2,054 NOTE, 3,080 MILESTONE, 4,730 HI, and 4 HEAD; separately counted 9,763/315, 2,492/68, and 1,280/29 L/LG in the three literary streams.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME LICENSE at commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c
evidence.summary: Contains the Unlicense wording and expressly describes the dedicated material as software; it does not separately define the rights status of corpus XML data and markup.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Digital Collections, CME00133 item page
evidence.summary: Provides the stable identity and public presentation for the U-M electronic text represented by the repository artifact.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse collection information
evidence.summary: Describes the collection's manually keyed public-domain-edition sources and supplies the No Copyright - United States rights statement used at the digital-encoding layer.

evidence.citation: Eugen Kölbing, ed., Arthour and Merlin, nach der Auchinleck-Hs. Nebst zwei Beilagen (Leipzig: O. R. Reisland, 1890)
evidence.locator: 1890 title page; introduction pp. IX-XIX; text pp. 1-370
evidence.summary: The complete source establishes Kölbing's direct Auchinleck, L, and D work; his abbreviation, capitalization, word-division, and collation practices; the L/P/H and D family model; L's missing leaf and termination; P supplies through Hales/Furnivall; and H readings through Turnbull.
notes: The cached scan is a 1968 Rodopi facsimile reissue that preserves the complete 1890 edition. Claims about the 1890 edition were checked against the original title and pagination inside that reissue.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for arthourmerlinnac00koeluoft
evidence.summary: Identifies the item, scan derivatives, and publication metadata; no licenseurl or rights field is supplied for the scan.

evidence.citation: National Library of Scotland catalogue, Advocates MS 19.2.1
evidence.summary: Dates the Auchinleck Manuscript to 1331-1340, associates its production probably with London, and describes its six-scribe organization.

evidence.citation: National Library of Scotland Auchinleck Manuscript, Arthour and Merlin headnote
evidence.summary: Identifies Scribe 1 and the work locus, explains 9,763 actual lines versus 9,938 numbered positions, describes damage and loss, and relates the NLS text to L, D, P, H, and Douce 124.

evidence.citation: NLS IIIF Presentation manifest for the Auchinleck Manuscript
evidence.summary: The exact 734-canvas manifest preserves object identity, canvases, image services, NLS attribution, and Public Domain Mark 1.0 rights evidence.

evidence.citation: Direct inspection of cached NLS Auchinleck canvases, 2026-07-13
evidence.summary: Confirmed that canvas 437/f. 201r contains the damaged opening in column b and canvas 548/f. 256v contains the ending, establishing the current folio-column range.

evidence.citation: Lincoln's Inn Digital Collections, Hale MS 150
evidence.summary: Exposes the complete 264-image page-turning object and broadly describes the codex as five early English metrical romances in a fourteenth-century hand.

evidence.citation: Direct inspection of the complete Hale MS 150 published-image inventory, 2026-07-13
evidence.summary: Confirmed the work opening and Explicit Merlin ending and established the image-level sequence supporting the legacy/current foliation crosswalk.

evidence.citation: Lincoln's Inn Digital Collections Terms of Use
evidence.summary: Asserts Lincoln's Inn ownership of site images and text, applies CC BY-NC-ND 4.0, limits ordinary reuse to non-commercial purposes, and supplies the requested attribution wording and commercial-contact route.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries medieval manuscript catalogue, MS Douce 236, commit-pinned TEI
evidence.summary: Identifies the imperfect Middle English romance, inferred item boundary, gap after f. 23, fifteenth-century English parchment codex, Dorset association, and three-image partial surrogate.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian IIIF manifest for MS Douce 236 selected surfaces
evidence.summary: The exact three-canvas manifest labels the selected openings, links the official catalogue, states Photo © Bodleian Libraries, and applies CC BY-NC 4.0.

evidence.citation: Direct inspection of the three cached Douce 236 surfaces, 2026-07-13
evidence.summary: Confirmed that all three public openings contain the target romance and that the object remains only partial coverage of the witness and work.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Special Collections mediated copying service
evidence.summary: Provides the current mediated-copying request route, eligibility, assessment, contact, fee basis, and service expectations for reference scans.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian terms of use
evidence.summary: Explains Digital Bodleian image rights and separates access from permissions applying to reuse and ordered images.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, using Special Collections
evidence.summary: Provides the current reader-registration, advance-ordering, and physical-consultation route.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Add MS 27879
evidence.summary: Identifies the Percy Folio, dates it 1630-1670, describes its paper support and English content, marks it not digitised, and states that a letter of introduction is required.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Harley MS 6223
evidence.locator: Arthour and Merline at current ff. 2r-2v
evidence.summary: Identifies John Stow's 62-line excerpt at current ff. 2r-2v, dates the paper miscellany 1550-1599, records 21 folios and English origin, and marks it not digitised.

evidence.citation: British Library Digitisation Services
evidence.summary: Provides the current made-to-order digitisation enquiry and quotation route for collection items, subject to condition, assessment, and licensing.

evidence.citation: J. W. Hales and F. J. Furnivall, eds., Bishop Percy's Folio Manuscript: Ballads and Romances, vol. 1 (London: N. Trübner, 1867-68)
evidence.locator: Arthur/Merlin component pp. 401-496; P transcript pp. 417-496; Kölbing-cited run pp. 422-496
evidence.summary: Provides the complete printed Percy Folio intermediary. Its Arthur/Merlin introduction occupies pp. 401-416, the MERLINE transcript pp. 417-496, and Kölbing uses/cites pp. 422-496 for P supplies and readings.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for bishoppercysfoli01percuoft
evidence.summary: Identifies the volume, 1867-68 publication, Robarts contributor, and scan derivatives; no licenseurl or rights field is supplied.

evidence.citation: W. B. D. D. Turnbull, ed., Arthour and Merlin: A Metrical Romance (Edinburgh: Abbotsford Club, 1838)
evidence.locator: Harley fragment pp. x-xiii; main Auchinleck edition
evidence.summary: Provides the printed intermediary for the Harley 6223 fragment and the earlier Auchinleck edition that Kölbing consulted and criticized without using as his direct base.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for arthourmerlinmet00abbo
evidence.summary: Identifies the 1838 item, NLS contributor, scan derivatives, and CC BY-NC-SA 2.5 Scotland licenseurl.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, version 1.0, records 2807 and 1886 and associated authority data
evidence.locator: Records.xml records 2807 and 1886; Manuscripts.xml witness entries; Bibliography.xml edition entries; LICENSE
evidence.summary: Supplies the two incipits and their cross-reference, IMEV/NIMEV identifiers, witness sigla and loci, line-count traditions, edition citations, linguistic profiles, and CC BY 4.0 release terms.
notes: DIMEV record 1003 is a distinct Wynkyn de Worde print cross-reference and is not modeled as an ancestor of CME00133.

open_questions.question: Did Kölbing directly inspect BL Add MS 27879 when correcting small errors in Hales/Furnivall, or did he work only from the printed transcript and other comparison evidence?
next_steps: Search Kölbing's correspondence, working papers, and any fuller publication history for an explicit Percy Folio inspection statement.
Retain the current Kölbing -> Hales/Furnivall -> P chain unless direct codex use is affirmatively documented.
notes: Kölbing's statement that he corrected small inaccuracies in the printed edition does not by itself prove direct manuscript consultation.

open_questions.question: Why does DIMEV count the Harley 6223 excerpt as 61 lines while the British Library catalogue and Kölbing describe 62?
next_steps: Obtain images of current ff. 2r-2v and compare physical lines with Turnbull's transcript and DIMEV's counting convention.

open_questions.question: How exactly does Kölbing/DIMEV's ff. 1-36v text boundary map to the current Bodleian catalogue's inferred ff. 1r-37v item boundary?
next_steps: Inspect a complete current folio sequence or request reference scans of ff. 36r-37v.
Compare historical and current foliation marks before asserting an exact crosswalk.
notes: The three public openings do not include the terminal leaves and cannot resolve the discrepancy.

open_questions.question: Is a complete public or privately requestable existing digital facsimile of MS Douce 236 available beyond the three selected Digital Bodleian openings?
next_steps: Ask Bodleian whether an existing internal surrogate can be supplied before commissioning new photography.
If none exists, request a quote for reference scans of the full codex or at minimum ff. 1r-37v.

open_questions.question: Is a complete public or privately requestable existing digital facsimile of BL Add MS 27879 available?
next_steps: Ask BL Digitisation Services whether legacy photography exists and can be supplied.
If necessary, request manuscript pp. 145-178 first and a separate whole-object quotation.

open_questions.question: Is a complete public or privately requestable existing digital facsimile of BL Harley MS 6223 available?
next_steps: Ask BL Digitisation Services whether legacy photography exists and can be supplied.
If necessary, request current ff. 2r-2v first and a separate whole-object quotation.

open_questions.question: How should the NLS headnote's c.1450 date for Hale 150 be reconciled with Lincoln's Inn's broad description of a fourteenth-century hand?
next_steps: Consult the full Lincoln's Inn manuscript catalogue and current palaeographical bibliography.
Continue to qualify the c.1450 date rather than flattening the conflict.

open_questions.question: Does the CME repository's Unlicense dedication expressly cover the corpus XML data and editorial markup, or only software?
next_steps: Seek a repository-level clarification or data-specific rights statement.
Keep the repository-artifact rights layer unknown while retaining separate public-domain and provider-rights assessments for source layers.
